evaluation.question: How can I create a new document using a template, apply strikethrough to a specific text, and then add a high-priority comment to that text?
evaluation.answer:
1. Click the 'File' section.
2. Click the New Document icon.
3. Choose the 'Document Type' you want (e.g., Blank Document, Business Letter, Project Report, Internal Memo, Personal Resume, Newsletter, Invoice).
4. Change the 'Document Title' to what you want (default: 'Untitled Document').
5. Click the 'Create Document' button.
6. Click the 'Insert' section.
7. Click the Template Library icon.
8. Click a template from the template library (e.g., Business Letter, Report, Resume), and the template will be inserted.
9. Click the 'Format' section.
10. Click the Strikethrough Format icon.
11. Select the text you want to strikethrough.
12. Click the 'Apply Strikethrough' button, and the text will have a line through it.
13. Click the 'Tools' section.
14. Click the New Comment icon.
15. Input the comment you want to add in the comment content field.
16. Input the author name in the comment author field.
17. Select 'High Priority' from the comment priority field.
18. Click 'Add Comment to Selected Text' button to save.
19. Select the text in the document you want to add the comment with the cursor.
20. Click the selected text in the document to view the added comment.

evaluation.answer:
1. Click the 'Insert' section.
2. Click the Template Library icon.
3. Click the 'Business Letter' template from the template library; the template will be inserted.
4. Click the 'Edit' section.
5. Click the Find and Replace icon.
6. Enter 'Dear Sir' in the text to find field.
7. Enter 'Dear Madam' in the replacement text field.
8. Click 'Replace All' button to replace all instances of 'Dear Sir' with 'Dear Madam'.
9. Click the 'Format' section.
10. Click the Justify Align icon.
11. Select the entire document by pressing 'Ctrl + A'.
12. Click the 'Apply Justify Align' button to justify align the text.
13. Click the 'View' section.
14. Click the Zoom In View icon.
15. Select '150%' as the zoom level.
16. Click the 'Apply Zoom In' button to magnify the document to 150%.
